entries.text: Telltale columns of dust promise the arrival of legions of demonic minions. After the squad has secured their resources, start a TACCOM encounter against a Swarm no fewer than 15 units in size, with an objective to reach an escape zone (the HELLPIERCER's vehicle or the boundary of the map). Place 3 Scrap Piles on the map; each rewards the players with 1 random resource if interacted with. After three turns, spawn another Swarm of 15 units.
Scavenging unearths something unexpected; roll on the Vaults table and incorporate the result into the search.

Once the salvage has been stowed, a dark shadow looms in the sky. Players may choose to escape now or to gain 1 additional resource. If they opt for the additional resource, the GM gains 3 Charge. They may spend that Charge at any time during the next TACCOM phase to spawn a miniboss from any faction.

The party sights an abandoned tomb ruined by the constant howling winds. Within is a sentient shadow, a remnant of one of the lost factions. It is willing to trade up to three of the resources it holds within its body in exchange for accompanying the party to their destination. If they agree, select a miniboss from any faction and allow the party to control it as a shared unit. If the shadow encounters a boss creature, it will attempt to consume it and take its Crown. What happens next is up to you.

Within the pile of scrap is a much larger construct, buried by centuries of dust/soil/rock/living fungus. It’s human, but it’s not one of yours. Within are a number of corpses in NASAstyle space suits, too decomposed to identify. Its fragmented logs describe something called THE SAMSON PROTOCOL, an undocumented incursion into Hell for unknown purposes. It looks like whoever these humans were, they failed. Create a TACCOM map with a wide open space, centered on a large wreck. The wreck has at least one Interact space marked, and one “wall” of the arena should be marked as the point of egress, but the map is otherwise empty until the wreck has been Interacted with by a player. As soon as the players interact with the wreck, spawn a massive horde of at least 10 units on an edge of the battlefield. Spawn another at the start of the next round, and another at the start of the round following. If players defeat the entire horde, they secure the wreck for transport back to DIS, where someone will help figure out what was going on here. Otherwise, they may escape via the egress tiles.

Resources here require the deployment of an ascension-blessed spinther ignition drill. Create a TACCOM map large enough to accommodate three small hills with an Interact spot at the top of each. When a player interacts with those spots, they place the Drill and begin converting the soil into liberated ascended humanity, creating an enormous golden veil of godsmoke. The drill gains Countdown:8, remove each drill that reaches 0. After the first hill is interacted with, begin spawning one Minion unit near the edge of the battlefield after each player turn and one miniboss at the end of each round. Each additional Drill placed spawns one additional minion per player turn and one additional Miniboss per round. If players activate all three drills, spawn one Boss-level creature at the end of the following round instead of three Minibosses. At the end of each drill’s countdown, players receive 4 Hellsteel, 4 Brimstone, or 4 Soulfire, and may opt to leave the battle immediately. If they complete all three drills, end TACCOM and roll on the Vaults table.

Fog rolls down a nearby embankment, wailing and carrying dreaded reality edits, pulling apart the local timespace. Several enemy battalions are using it as cover. While the player squad’s extractor runs, create a TACCOM map with a defensible location on one side, the Base, where the players spawn. The rest of the map is a flat open space. Draw a line across the map around two rows ahead of the base. Players may not pass this line. At the end of each round, the fog covers the furthest uncovered row from the Base, advancing toward the line drawn earlier. When the fog reaches that line, the extractor finishes its work. Players cannot see through the fog, cannot use any abilities that reveal enemies under the fog, etc. The GM may spawn units as they see fit, most likely a minion heavy mix from the current faction.

A rich source of fuel and supplies erupts from a volcanic geyser nearby. Enemy faction units are attempting to exploit the same resources as the players here too. Create a TACCOM map with a large volcano in the center, occupying a roughly 3x3 space. At the start of every round, select a tile at random by rolling one die for the row and one for the column. The volcano launches a 1x1 fireball from the center of the volcano toward the random tile. It moves 1 space at the end of each turn (player or GM) until it arrives at its destination. The fireball deals 6 damage to any unit it collides but otherwise does not impede movement. When the fireball arrives at its destination, it creates a Blast:2, 5 damage explosion that converts all spaces within the blast to Scorch terrain. Scorch terrain deals 1 damage when a unit leaves that space. To win, the players must interact with the Volcano three times. If an enemy faction Interacts with the Volcano, launch a fireball at one player character’s current location. When anyone Interacts with the Volcano, it gains Cooldown:1, preventing anyone else from Interacting with it until the next round.

A network of chasms and canyons blocks lines of sight and egress. The survey auguries have detected veins of hardened soulmatter within. Deeper into the labyrinthian geography, the walls of nousbound stone grow pliant, malleable, soft like melting plastic. After the players find the source of the readings and begin extraction, alien claws grasp from within the gloopy rock. Demons from factions not actively in play tear their way into this reality. Create a TACCOM scene with lots of long corridors branching off from a large central arena. These corridors terminate in an Escape tile that allows players to leave the battle when interacted with. Pick 4 or 5 spawners from a variety of factions and place them within the walls of the canyon. Spawn units out of the boundaries of the map and the walls.

On a hellswept bluff, twin stone obelisks mark a set of stairs leading deep into the bowels of Hell. At the foot of the stairs rests a wide, open chamber, lit with an unearthly light. Its low ceiling is decorated in images of the divine feminine, while the center of the room is taken up by an enormous summoning circle bearing the sign of the ouroboros. If a player steps into this circle, they appear on the frontlines of a vast demonic horde. Have all players who stepped in the circle select a demonic faction, and a unit from that faction. They are experiencing a temporal vision of the pivotal moment when that faction rose to power, claiming their crown and their sovereignty. With some effort, players can leap between creatures in this realm, with minions being easy to co-opt and bosses nigh impossible. During any TACCOM scenes, have players use enemy units and allow them to change to another enemy unit as a Support Action or when their current host dies. Players leave with their new insights when it is narratively dramatic for them to do so.
